NATHAN LITTAUER HOSPITAL NURSING HOME

CCN: 335351 · GLOVERSVILLE, NY 12078 · Fulton County

Overview

Address
99 EAST STATE STREET, GLOVERSVILLE, NY 12078

Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.26 3.92 4.20 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.92 0.69 0.90 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.14 0.87 1.13 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.20 2.36 2.16 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.06 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.61
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.47
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.56
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
61.0%
annual
RN turnover
40.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.39
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.01
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-19 15 15 3 92 1 92
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-05-06 5 5 0 28 1 28
Total weighted health score
76.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (25)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
23 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly E 2025-02-19 2025-03-31
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2022-05-06 2022-07-01
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-05-06 2022-07-01
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-05-06 2022-07-01
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-05-06 2022-07-01
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2022-05-06 2022-07-01
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. C 2019-12-10 2020-01-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-12-10 2020-01-23
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2019-12-10 2020-01-23
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2019-12-10 2020-01-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. C 2019-12-10 2020-01-23
